title: Travel Policy
text:
# Company Travel Policy

All domestic travel must be pre-approved by your direct manager at least 5 business days before departure.

## Hotel Reimbursement

Employees are reimbursed up to $75 per night for hotel stays during domestic travel. International travel allows up to $150 per night. Receipts are required for all hotel expenses.

## Flights

All flights should be booked through ConcurTravel. Economy class only for flights under 6 hours. Business class requires VP-level approval for flights over 6 hours.

## Meals

Daily meal allowance is $50 for domestic travel and $75 for international travel. Alcohol is not reimbursable.

title: Expense Guidelines
text:
# Expense Guidelines for Employees

## Travel Expenses

Domestic travel requires manager approval prior to booking. Use Concur for all flight bookings. Economy class is required for flights shorter than 6 hours.

The per-night hotel allowance for domestic travel is $100, receipts required. International hotel stays are capped at $200 per night.

## Meal Expenses

Daily meal allowance is $60 for domestic travel. International meal allowance is $75. All meals must be documented with itemized receipts.

## Expense Submission

All expense reports must be submitted within 14 days of travel completion through the Concur platform.

title: Security Policy
text:
# Security Policy

Last updated: March 15, 2021

## Incident Reporting

All security incidents should be reported to CTO John Doe at john.doe@company.com within 24 hours of discovery.

## Access Control

As of Q1 2021, all employees must use two-factor authentication for accessing company systems. Our SOC2 certification is maintained through vendor AuditCorp.

## Password Policy

Passwords must be at least 8 characters and changed every 90 days. Password managers are recommended but not required.

## Remote Access

VPN access requires approval from the IT department. Currently, we use CiscoVPN version 4.2 for all remote connections.

title: Employee Benefits Guide
text:
# Employee Benefits Guide

## Health Insurance

All full-time employees are eligible for health insurance coverage. Coverage begins on the first day of employment. We offer three plan tiers: Basic, Standard, and Premium.

## Dental Coverage

Dental coverage begins after a 90-day waiting period from your start date. Coverage includes preventive care, basic procedures, and major procedures with varying copay levels.

## Vision Coverage

Vision coverage is included in all health plan tiers. Annual eye exams and a frames allowance of $200 are included.

## 401k

The company matches 401k contributions up to 4% of your salary. Vesting is immediate for all employees.

title: New Hire FAQ
text:
# New Hire FAQ

## When do my benefits start?

All benefits, including dental, vision, and health insurance, are effective from your first day of employment. No waiting period is required for any benefit.

## How do I enroll?

Log into the HR portal at hr.company.com within your first week. Select your preferred health plan tier and add any dependents.

## What about 401k?

You can enroll in the 401k program immediately. The company matches up to 4% of your salary with immediate vesting.

## Who do I contact for help?

Reach out to the HR team at hr@company.com or visit the HR portal for self-service options.

title: Code Review Guidelines
text:
# Code Review Guidelines

## Purpose

Code reviews ensure code quality, share knowledge across the team, and catch bugs before they reach production. Every pull request requires at least one approval before merging.

## Review Expectations

Reviewers should provide feedback within 24 hours of being assigned. Focus on correctness, readability, test coverage, and adherence to our style guide. Be constructive and specific in feedback.

## What to Look For

Check for proper error handling, edge cases, security implications, and performance considerations. Ensure tests cover the new functionality and that documentation is updated if APIs change.

## Approval and Merging

Once approved, the PR author is responsible for merging. Squash commits for feature branches. Use conventional commit messages.
